title: hieracium glabratum subsp. acrovillosum
intro: Hieracium glabratum subsp. acrovillosum, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family, with yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Hawkweed also offer health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and antioxidant effects. Hawkweed can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used externally to soothe skin irritations and promote healing.
